	modified:   .gitignore
- Current git diff (staged and unstaged changes): <persisted-output>
Output too large (139.8KB). Full output saved to: /home/an-lee/.claude/projects/-home-an-lee-projects-ghr/f4151d1c-5c30-43fb-9a47-4d32f244ba05/tool-results/bd1096l2n.txt

Preview (first 2KB):
diff --git a/.github/workflows/release.yml b/.github/workflows/release.yml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f0ef850
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/workflows/release.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+name: Release
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - "v*"
+
+permissions:
+  contents: write
+
+jobs:
+  release:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: cli/gh-extension-precompile@v2
+        with:
+          go_version_file: go.mod
diff --git a/.gitignore b/.gitignore
index fb7e972..4a76833 100644
--- a/.gitignore
+++ b/.gitignore
@@ -1,8 +1,9 @@
 .env
-/ghr
+/gh-wm
 dist/
 site/
 docs/public/
 docs/resources/
 docs/themes/
 docs/.hugo_build.lock
+ghr
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 7b26e44..4ccdabb 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
-# ghr — GitHub Actions runner manager CLI
-# https://github.com/an-lee/ghr
+# gh-wm — GitHub Workflow Manager (GitHub CLI extension)
+# https://github.com/an-lee/gh-wm
 
 ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
-BINARY := ghr.exe
+BINARY := gh-wm.exe
 else
-BINARY := ghr
+BINARY := gh-wm
 endif
 
-CMD_DIR := ./cmd/ghr
+CMD_DIR := ./cmd/gh-wm
 
 GIT_TAG := $(shell git describe --tags --always --dirty 2>/dev/null || echo dev)
 
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 0e42117..cf8cfe4 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,21 +1,21 @@
-# ghr
+# GitHub Workflow Manager (`gh wm`)
 
-**ghr** is a CLI for managing self-hosted [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com/en/actions) runners across many machines from one place—usually your laptop. It connects over **SSH** (or runs **locally** with `addr: local`), installs and starts runners, and gives you a **terminal UI** for day-to-day operations.
...
</persisted-output>
- Current branch: main
- Recent commits: 798df09 feat: agentic workflows support (profile, org runners, ephemeral)
9f873b8 feat: auto-detect runners, smart up/add/init, and docker_cap_add for gh-aw
dfa72b7 feat: allow docker_network_mode: host on Windows and macOS hosts
4b73a92 feat: docker_network_mode host for Linux docker runners (gh-aw)
c639a71 feat(doctor): check Windows Docker socket GID and container --group-add flags
875521f fix(runner): include --group-add 0 for root-owned Docker socket on Windows
6c661b9 feat: auto-resolve Docker socket for Colima and rootless
577f27a fix(runner): add Docker socket GID probe for Windows docker-mode
ae2f965 fix(runner): mount Docker socket for docker-mode on macOS and Windows
24969cd fix: remove duplicate help command shadowing cobra's built-in

## Your task

Based on the above changes, create a single git commit.

You have the capability to call multiple tools in a single response. Stage and create the commit using a single message. Do not use any other tools or do anything else. Do not send any other text or messages besides these tool calls.

addedBlocks:
## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

error.message: The service may be temporarily overloaded, please try again later

content.thinking: Let me analyze the changes to create an appropriate commit message. Looking at the diff preview, this is a major rename/rebrand from `ghr` to `gh-wm` (GitHub Workflow Manager), transforming it into a GitHub CLI extension. Let me read more of the diff to understand the full scope.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: rebrand ghr to gh-wm GitHub CLI extension

Rename the project from ghr to gh-wm (GitHub Workflow Manager), restructuring
it as a gh CLI extension (gh wm). Updates binary name, command paths, module
references, config paths, documentation, and adds a release workflow using
cli/gh-extension-precompile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
